metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `progressive_drift_refresh` inlining anchor: specify that `orchestrator.drift_refresh(...)` replaces the `if sign and _progressive_enabled(repo_root):` block at line 492, keeping it AFTER the idempotent-reuse early-return at line 483-486 (i.e., `if sign: refreshed = orchestrator.drift_refresh(...)`).
findings.finding: The inlining instruction for `progressive_drift_refresh` is ambiguous about which `if sign` guard to use as the anchor: there are two in scope (line 483 and line 492), and an executor following 'Inline the `drift_refresh` call under the existing `if sign` guard' could place the call inside the idempotent-reuse block (wrong) rather than sequentially after it (correct).
findings.location: What section — `progressive_drift_refresh` bullet: 'Inline the `drift_refresh` call under the existing `if sign` guard'; `llm/plan_review/__init__.py:483-497`
findings.suggested_fix: Change the bullet to: 'Delete `_progressive_enabled` and replace the `if sign and _progressive_enabled(repo_root):` block (:492-497) with `if sign:` directly — keeping it sequentially after the idempotent-reuse early-return (:483-486) and before the remediation decision (:503).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback plan (e.g., a named revert commit, an emergency config key, or a documented expand-contract sequence) so that if always-on behavior causes a regression post-merge, the team has a stated recovery path that does not require a fresh feature implementation.
findings.finding: The plan removes the only operator-accessible mechanism to disable `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, and `novelty_drop_active` (the config flags themselves were the back-out path), but provides no alternative rollback path if always-on behavior causes problems post-deployment. The plan's 'Why' section even calls the `false` value a 'back-out', making the removal of the rollback mechanism explicit — yet no substitute rollback mechanism (e.g., a revert commit strategy, a feature branch hold, an emergency config key, or an expand-contract phase) is described.
findings.location: Why section ('false value being only a back-out'); AC line 1 ('verify no stragglers'); Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If always-on behavior regresses, revert this commit via `git revert <sha>`; the flags and their false-path branches are fully restored by the revert. No data migration is needed.' This is sufficient to satisfy T4's reversibility requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (or implement) how the config parser handles the three deleted keys if they appear in operator-supplied config overlays — either confirm `extra='ignore'` behavior, add a deprecation-warning shim, or explicitly scope the 'verify no stragglers' check to ALL config sources, not just rebar.toml.
findings.finding: The plan specifies checking `rebar.toml` for straggler flag values but does not address what happens at runtime if an operator's deployed config overlay (outside `rebar.toml`) still contains one of the three deleted keys — whether the config parser will reject the config (breaking deployments) or silently ignore the unknown fields.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The three flags removed from schema + parser + rebar.toml (none currently set there, so verify no stragglers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the plan confirming the VerifyConfig parser's behavior for unknown keys (e.g., 'Pydantic model uses extra=ignore so stale keys are silently dropped'). If the parser is strict, add a one-release deprecation shim that accepts and warns on the old keys before removing them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add docs/config.md to the edit set and remove (or replace with a deprecation note for) the three removed flag entries at lines 100, 110–114, and 121–124.
findings.finding: docs/config.md documents all three removed flags as operator-settable with explicit `false` back-out syntax (lines 100, 110–114, 121–124). Post-removal these keys become unknown (silently ignored or hard-error under REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error), but the plan's Scope section and AC do not enumerate docs/config.md as a file to update, leaving the user-facing reference stale.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the three flag entries from docs/config.md. Optionally, add a one-liner note in the comments of the retained tuning params (novelty_drop_threshold etc.) that the on/off gate is now hardcoded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and update (at minimum with an 'Update:' note) the ADR and narrative doc files that describe the now-removed `false` back-out paths: docs/adr/0002, docs/adr/0008, docs/adr/0037, docs/plan-review-gate.md, docs/review-kernel.md.
findings.finding: Multiple ADR documents describe the `false` back-out paths for all three flags as an operator capability: adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:93, adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:90, adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md:92, plan-review-gate.md:344/377/381, review-kernel.md:124. These unenumerated files will document a no-longer-existing operator switch post-change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-g7.eval.yaml to the cleanup scope and update or replace the fixture at line 57 that references the removed progressive_drift_refresh field.
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-g7.eval.yaml:57 contains a G7 eval corpus fixture whose input string references 'Flip verify.progressive_drift_refresh to default True in _config_schema.py' as a task description. Post-removal the field no longer exists, making this eval fixture describe a non-existent config key. This file is not listed in AC#3's test file set.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the G7-P-consistent-narrowing fixture input with an analogous example that doesn't reference the now-removed flag (e.g., describe flipping a different still-present tuning param).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Scope bullet or AC item requiring that `docs/config.md`, `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md`, and `docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md` be updated to remove or retract the three deleted flags and their explicit-false back-out documentation.
findings.finding: The plan scopes documentation updates to tests and source code only, but docs/config.md (lines 100, 110–124), docs/plan-review-gate.md (lines 344, 377, 381), and the two ADRs (0002, 0008) all document the three flags with their explicit-false back-out semantics. After deletion these docs describe a config knob that no longer exists and an operator escape hatch that no longer works — misleading anyone consulting them post-change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'UPDATE docs: remove `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, and `novelty_drop_active` entries from `docs/config.md`; retract the explicit-false back-out instructions in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (lines ~344, ~377, ~381) and the two ADRs (0002 line 93; 0008 line 90).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to leaf AC (or explicitly name a sibling story) that docs/config.md, docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md, and any other doc/help/release-note references to the three removed flags are deleted or rewritten — satisfying the parent epic's 'Doc-accuracy sweep' workstream and AC#4.
findings.finding: The leaf plan is silent on documentation cleanup for the three removed flags, but the parent epic includes an explicit 'Doc-accuracy sweep' workstream and AC#4 requires a repo-wide grep confirming no doc/help/release-note references remain for any removed config key. Existing references in docs/config.md:100 and docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md:64,93 mention progressive_drift_refresh with back-out instructions. The leaf neither claims this doc work nor defers it to a named sibling story, leaving a gap against the parent's AC#4.
findings.location: Leaf plan — Scope section / AC#1; Parent AC#4 and 'Doc-accuracy sweep' workstream
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: '- [ ] Repo-wide grep for `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, and `novelty_drop_active` in `docs/`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and help strings returns zero hits; update `docs/config.md` and `docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md` to remove back-out instructions for the deleted flags.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a backward-compat unknown-key test (following the `test_config_backcompat.py` pattern) asserting that a `rebar.toml` still containing any of the three removed flags produces a warning and is ignored (not a crash), and hard-errors under `REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error`.
findings.finding: The test-update AC (AC#3) specifies removing/rewriting false-path and default-True tests but omits adding a backward-compat unknown-key test for the three removed config fields. The repo's established pattern (test_config_backcompat.py) requires a test verifying that stale operator `rebar.toml` entries (`progressive_drift_refresh = false`, `remediation_mode = false`, `novelty_drop_active = false`) produce the correct warn-and-ignore behavior after schema deletion — the same protocol used for the removed `require_verdict_for_close` alias.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC#3: 'False-path tests removed/rewritten … default-True assertions dropped'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
